location.office_time_required: occasionally required to work out of assigned Affirm office for proximal roles

level_signals: Base pay range $169K–$240K depending on location, consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years in Product Management; Ideally with previous background owning growth/distribution channels
Track record shipping consumer financial or regulated products; comfort partnering with Risk/Credit, Legal, and Compliance.
Deep fluency in experiment design, incrementality/attribution, and CAC→LTV optimization.
Strong UX instincts for moment‑of‑intent surfaces; ability to work across web, app, and partner integrations.
Data chops (SQL/Amplitude‑level self‑serve) and a bias for shipping, learning, and iterating quickly.
Excellent cross‑functional leadership; experience leading embedded eng/design pods.
Bachelor's or Master's in Computer Science, or other related fields; or equivalent practical experience.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years in Product Management; Ideally with previous background owning growth/distribution channels

scope.surface: Affirm Card acquisition — new growth channels (referrals, partner pilots, web/app onboarding surfaces)
core_responsibilities: Define and own the Card Acquisition roadmap for new growth channels, setting clear hypotheses, success metrics, and sequencing.
Launch and scale new acquisition surfaces, partnering closely with Engineering and Design to deliver seamless, high-conversion onboarding experiences.
Design and execute acquisition programs (e.g., referrals, exploratory partner pilots) with measurable CAC→LTV impact.
Own funnel performance end-to-end: track weekly acquisitions, 30-day activation, drop-offs by step, and implement robust incrementality testing.
Collaborate cross-functionally with Risk, Compliance, Marketing, and the Card Foundations team to align dependencies and ensure safe, scalable growth.
Continuously experiment and iterate to unlock new distribution levers, maximize conversion, and expand the cardholder base.

compensation.equity_form: Equity Grade - 8; ESPP (employee stock purchase plan at a discount); equity rewards mentioned as part of total compensation

green_flags: Compensation range fully disclosed with geographic tiers ($169K–$240K base)
Remote-first company with broad US remote eligibility
Highly visible role with direct ownership over acquisition and activation metrics
100% subsidized medical coverage for employee and dependents
Flexible spending wallets and ESPP offered
Clear product surface and measurable outcome ownership
red_flags: Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for non-US-authorized candidates
Role title in identity fields is 'Card Experience' but JD body describes 'Card Acquisition (New Channels)' — possible mismatch or umbrella title
Pay grade listed as 'N' without explanation — internal grade system not transparent externally
Proximal role employees may occasionally be required in office — not fully clear whether this role is proximal or fully remote

ambiguities: Role title in identity fields is 'Senior Product Manager, Card Experience' but the JD body refers to 'Senior Product Manager, Card Acquisition (New Channels)' — may be a broader team umbrella title vs. specific sub-role title.
The JD says '#LI-Remote' and 'remote-first' but also notes proximal roles may need occasional office attendance — unclear if this specific role is classified as fully remote or proximal.
